pages.raw_text:
misunderstanding of report
rendered the Central Labor union
by the Garments union is RESPONSE
BILE for a statement yesterday that
the Traction Co. had raised wages.
The contrary is true. The Trac-
ton employees, including carmen,
electricians and all other classes of
labor on the payroll, have accepted
a reduction of 10 per cent in wages
on promise from the Graves
management that the old scale will
be resumed as soon as conditions
warrant. The same rule applies
equally to the men on the Occur
d'Alene and Inland systems.


What the carmen really gained
was a closed shop rule, or agree
ment that none but union men
would be employed.


The Graves street carmen have
been receiving rs cents an hour at
starting and 80 cents after three
years service. The passenger
trainmen on the interurban lines
have been paid 35 cents an hour.
